op.name: polyint
op.summary: Return an antiderivative (indefinite integral) of a polynomial.

blocks.text: Return an antiderivative (indefinite integral) of a polynomial.

blocks.name: note
blocks.body:
This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.
blocks.raw:
.. note::
   This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.

blocks.text:
The returned order `m` antiderivative `P` of polynomial `p` satisfies
:math:`\frac{d^m}{dx^m}P(x) = p(x)` and is defined up to `m - 1`
integration constants `k`. The constants determine the low-order
polynomial part

desc_blocks.text:
Polynomial to integrate.
A sequence is interpreted as polynomial coefficients, see `poly1d`.

items.name: k
items.type: list of `m` scalars or scalar, optional

desc_blocks.text:
Integration constants. They are given in the order of integration:
those corresponding to highest-order terms come first.

If ``None`` (default), all constants are assumed to be zero.
If `m = 1`, a single scalar can be given instead of a list.

blocks.text: The defining property of the antiderivative:

blocks.code:
>>> p = np.poly1d([1,1,1])
>>> P = np.polyint(p)
>>> P
 poly1d([ 0.33333333,  0.5       ,  1.        ,  0.        ]) # may vary
>>> np.polyder(P) == p
True

blocks.text: The integration constants default to zero, but can be specified:

Note that 3 = 6 / 2!, and that the constants are given in the order of
integrations. Constant of the highest-order polynomial term comes first:
